title: Fixes bug whereby read_only bool is called as a function
body:
This is a simple bug fix to remedy the following issue:

```python
  File ".../site-packages/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py", line 374, in emacs_insert_mode
    or app.current_buffer.read_only()
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Exception 'bool' object is not callable
```

It would appear to be a simple typo, calling the boolean `read_only` property as if it were a function.

files_modified.filename: tests/ (add or update a relevant test module)

step_plan.summary: Fixes a crash in application-level filter conditions by making checks of `current_buffer.read_only` resilient when it is a plain boolean rather than a callable Filter. Updates all identified mode/condition functions in `filters/app.py` that currently call `read_only()` so they work with both representations.

steps.operation: Harden read-only evaluation in emacs_insert_mode by safely handling both callable Filter and plain bool
steps.reason: The reported crash occurs when `app.current_buffer.read_only` is a bool and the code unconditionally calls it as `read_only()`. This condition must evaluate read-only status without assuming callability.
steps.side_effects: Changes behavior only in environments where `read_only` is not a Filter; should preserve existing behavior when it is callable. Add/adjust tests to ensure no exception and correct boolean result.

steps.operation: Make is_read_only robust to bool-backed read_only by using the same callable-vs-bool evaluation pattern
steps.reason: `is_read_only` is a shared condition used by other filters/key bindings; if it assumes `read_only()` it can trigger the same TypeError elsewhere. Centralizing the robust evaluation here also provides a reusable pattern.
steps.side_effects: May affect any code path relying on `is_read_only` by preventing crashes; expected behavior should remain identical for Filter-backed `read_only`.

steps.operation: Update vi_navigation_mode to avoid unconditionally calling current_buffer.read_only()
steps.reason: This condition is another direct caller of `app.current_buffer.read_only()` and is therefore another potential crash site in the same environments that produce the reported stack trace.
steps.side_effects: Ensures vi navigation mode condition evaluation never raises due to read_only shape; should not change semantics when `read_only` is callable.

steps.operation: Update vi_insert_mode to evaluate buffer read-only status safely (callable Filter vs bool)
steps.reason: Like other vi mode conditions, it guards on `current_buffer.read_only()` and can fail when `read_only` is a boolean. Keeping all vi-related conditions consistent avoids fragmented behavior.
steps.side_effects: Prevents TypeError during vi insert mode condition checks; may require updating/adding tests around vi mode conditions if they exist and assert exact call patterns.

steps.operation: Update vi_insert_multiple_mode to handle bool-backed read_only without calling it
steps.reason: This condition is also a direct call site of `read_only()`; applying the same robust evaluation reduces the chance of similar crashes in alternate vi submodes.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change expected for standard Filter-backed buffers; improves compatibility with older/alternate Buffer implementations.

steps.operation: Update vi_replace_mode to evaluate read-only safely (do not assume callable)
steps.reason: Another direct call site; ensuring all vi replace-related checks are robust prevents intermittent failures depending on buffer configuration.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change internal evaluation order if refactored, but resulting boolean should match prior behavior in the callable case.

steps.operation: Update vi_replace_single_mode to evaluate read-only safely (call when callable, else bool)
steps.reason: Final vi mode condition identified as calling `read_only()`; updating it completes the audit so all known app-level filters are tolerant of bool `read_only`.
steps.side_effects: Prevents TypeError when switching into single-replace mode with bool-backed buffers; should not impact other filters except by avoiding exceptions.
